commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared event-session routing helper and wires exec, cron, node-event, heartbeat, ACP parent-stream, and CLI-watchdog event enqueue/wake paths to route single-owner `session.dmScope=main` direct-message events to the agent main session.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source shows exec events preserve direct-DM keys without consulting `session.dmScope`, and the PR body describes a concrete direct-DM queue versus main-session drain mismatch; I did not run a fresh repro because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair is indicated; maintainers need a current-head proof refresh and a merge-risk decision before merge.

target.title: fix(core): route dmScope main event wakes to agent main
target.body:
Fixes #71581.
Replaces #72648 with a narrower implementation.

## Problem
When `session.dmScope` is set to `main`, OpenClaw can still receive internal exec/cron event wakes carrying an old direct-DM session key such as `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`. Those events are enqueued and woken on a queue the main heartbeat no longer drains, so completion notifications can sit unseen until another action happens to wake the right session.

## Change and value
This PR adds one shared event-session routing helper and uses it anywhere internal exec/cron event enqueue or heartbeat wake targets are resolved. Single-owner `dmScope=main` direct-DM event keys now route back to the drained agent main session, while existing cron-run remapping and explicit route-binding direct-session overrides remain intact.

## Who is affected, and who is not
- Affected: operators using `session.dmScope=main` with a single static DM owner allowlist, especially exec completion and cron-event wake flows.
- Unchanged: multi-owner allowlists, wildcard allowlists, non-agent node fallback sessions, route-binding direct-session overrides, and existing cron-run global/main remapping behavior.

## Why now
The original fix attempt in #72648 has stalled, but #71581 is still open and the current routing behavior still leaves a real wake/enqueue mismatch for main-scoped DM sessions. This replacement keeps the fix narrower and adds regression coverage around the shared routing boundary.

## Implementation
- Add `src/infra/event-session-routing.ts` for resolving event enqueue keys and heartbeat wake options from the same policy.
- Apply the helper to exec completion events, gateway node exec events, cron forced-session resolution, heartbeat forced-session resolution, ACP parent-stream events, and CLI watchdog events.
- Cover the new behavior in routing, exec runtime, heartbeat, and node event tests.

## Duplicate check
Before opening this PR, I searched open PRs for `71581 dmScope exec-event cron-event heartbeat`, `session.dmScope heartbeat`, and `resolveHeartbeatSession resolveEventSessionKey scopedHeartbeatWakeOptions`. No active duplicate appeared other than the abandoned #72648.

## Verification
- Local: `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- Local: `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- Local: `git diff --check`
- Local: real background exec proof and real forced cron-run proof in the `## Real behavior proof` section below.
- Remote GCP Crabbox Spot VM, `e2-standard-4`, `us-east1-b`, commit `8a2a08a47f`:

```sh
OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 pnpm check:changed -- --timed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts \
  src/infra/event-session-routing.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.subagent-session-guard.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
```

Remote result: `check:changed` clean; targeted Vitest `5 passed`, `126 passed`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed:
- With `session.dmScope=main` and a single static DM owner allowlist, internal exec/cron event wakes can carry an old direct DM session key such as `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`. Those events need to enqueue and wake the agent main session that the heartbeat actually drains.

Real environment tested:
- Local PR-head checkout at commit `8a2a08a47f`
- Node `24.15.0`
- Real production exec modules exercised: `runExecProcess`, background exec completion notification, `enqueueSystemEvent`, `requestHeartbeat`, the heartbeat wake scheduler, and `drainSystemEventEntries`
- Real production cron modules exercised: `buildGatewayCronService`, `CronService`, forced cron run dispatch, `enqueueSystemEvent`, `requestHeartbeat`, the heartbeat wake scheduler, and `drainSystemEventEntries`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Exec path: reset heartbeat wake state and clear both the old direct-DM queue `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123` and the agent main queue `agent:main:main`.
2. Exec path: register the real heartbeat wake handler. The handler records the wake request, drains the old direct-DM queue, and drains the agent main queue.
3. Exec path: start a real background exec with `runExecProcess`, command `sleep 0.2; printf proof-output`, `notifyOnExit: true`, session key `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`, and routing policy from config `{ session: { dmScope: "main" }, channels: { telegram: { allowFrom: ["123"] } } }`.
4. Exec path: mark the exec session backgrounded, wait for the real process completion, then wait for the real heartbeat wake scheduler to call the registered handler.
5. Exec path: fail the proof if the exec did not complete, if the heartbeat wake did not target `agent:main:main`, if the old direct-DM queue drained any events, or if the main queue did not drain exactly one exec completion event.
6. Cron path: reset heartbeat wake state and clear both queues again.
7. Cron path: install a PR-head runtime config with `session.dmScope = "main"`, a single Telegram allowlist owner `"123"`, and an isolated temporary cron store.
8. Cron path: build the real gateway cron service with `buildGatewayCronService`, add a main-session system-event cron job using session key `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`, then run it with `cron.run(job.id, "force")`.
9. Cron path: wait for the real heartbeat wake scheduler, then fail the proof if the forced cron run did not complete, if the heartbeat wake did not target `agent:main:main`, if the old direct-DM queue drained any events, or if the main queue did not drain exactly one cron event.

Evidence after fix:
Copied live exec transcript from the PR-head checkout:
```json
{
  "startSessionKey": "agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123",
  "execOutcome": "completed",
  "heartbeatWake": {
    "source": "exec-event",
    "intent": "event",
    "reason": "exec-event",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main"
  },
  "directQueueDrainedCount": 0,
  "mainQueueDrainedCount": 1,
  "mainEventText": "Exec completed (<exec-id>, code 0) :: proof-output"
}
```

Copied live cron transcript from the PR-head checkout:
```json
{
  "startSessionKey": "agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123",
  "cronJobId": "7699c3eb-caba-45b8-b442-b1297920f04f",
  "cronRunOk": true,
  "heartbeatWake": {
    "source": "cron",
    "intent": "event",
    "reason": "cron:7699c3eb-caba-45b8-b442-b1297920f04f",
    "agentId": "main",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "heartbeat": {
      "target": "last"
    }
  },
  "directQueueDrainedCount": 0,
  "mainQueueDrainedCount": 1,
  "mainEventText": "cron-proof-output"
}
```

Observed result after fix:
- The real background exec started from the old direct-DM session key `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`.
- The exec completion path enqueued no event to the old direct-DM queue.
- The heartbeat wake scheduler fired an `exec-event` wake for `agent:main:main`.
- The main-session drain consumed exactly one exec completion event: `Exec completed (<exec-id>, code 0) :: proof-output`.
- The real forced cron run started from the old direct-DM session key `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`.
- The cron system-event path enqueued no event to the old direct-DM queue.
- The heartbeat wake scheduler fired a `cron` wake for `agent:main:main`.
- The main-session drain consumed exactly one cron event: `cron-proof-output`.

What was not tested:
- A full local WSL Vitest run; local Vitest stalled before output on this host, so broad validation was run remotely.
- A live Telegram/Discord transport run. The proof exercises the real background exec and forced cron event/wake/drain paths without sending an external chat message.
- Live ACP parent-stream, gateway node-event, and CLI-watchdog runtime runs. These callers use the same shared event-session routing helper and are covered by targeted unit/regression tests, but the live proof above focuses on the two operator-facing event sources from #71581: exec completion and cron events.
- `pnpm check:changed` without `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1`; the raw VM previously hung in unrelated plugin SDK boundary-artifact lint preparation, so the final remote gate skipped only that preparation step while still running full core lint.
target.author: kaspre
